message:
fix(indexer): remove cross-database rowid watermark comparison and force autoindex on message fetches

The full-rebuild logic previously compared MAX(messages.id) across
separate SQLite files to decide whether the canonical database was
"behind" a historical bundle and needed discarding. This produced
false positives because message IDs are local row identifiers with
no cross-database ordering guarantee — a freshly-rebuilt canonical
DB could have lower rowids than a historical bundle while containing
more actual data.

indexer/mod.rs:
- Remove best_historical_bundle_message_watermark import and all
  cross-database watermark comparison logic from
  full_rebuild_requires_historical_restart()
- The function now never discards a healthy canonical database —
  rusqlite health probe failures and quick_check failures preserve
  the already-open database instead of triggering a restart
- Add canonical_only_full_rebuild parameter to
  should_salvage_historical_databases() to allow callers to skip
  historical salvage when only canonical data is needed
- Add tracing::warn! for historical salvage decisions with all
  relevant variables for debugging
- New test: historical_salvage_decision_skips_pending_bundles_
  during_canonical_only_full_rebuild
- New test: full_rebuild_restart_ignores_stale_progress_when_
  canonical_is_healthy
- Renamed test: full_rebuild_does_not_restart_based_on_historical_
  local_rowids (assertion flipped to false)

storage/sqlite.rs:
- Delete best_historical_bundle_message_watermark() — no longer
  needed with cross-database comparison removed
- Add INDEXED BY sqlite_autoindex_messages_1 hint to fetch_messages,
  fetch_messages_for_lexical_rebuild, and fetch_messages_batch to
  force the (conversation_id, idx) autoindex and prevent full table
  scans during rebuilds
- New test: lexical_rebuild_messages_query_forces_conversation_idx_
  autoindex verifies EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN uses the expected index

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
